expectations.text: No task directory, ticket list, or task files were created in outputs/. The only file present is transcript.md.
expectations.evidence: Filesystem listing of with_skill/outputs/ shows only transcript.md (2283 bytes); no tasks-*/ directory or ticket files were created.

expectations.text: The transcript explicitly REFUSES to treat the PRD as a TDD and to break it down.
expectations.evidence: Opens with 'I won't break this down without a TDD — without one, the tasks would be my opinions about a feature, not a decomposition of a design the team has agreed on...' — an explicit refusal.

expectations.text: The transcript explicitly distinguishes a PRD from a TDD: a PRD states the problem; a TDD states the design. A breakdown decomposes a design, not a problem.
expectations.evidence: 'it is a PRD: it states the problem, the goals, user stories, and constraints, but it does not state the design. A task breakdown decomposes a *design*, and there isn't one here yet.'

expectations.text: The transcript explicitly offers to author the TDD FIRST via the same skill's TDD-authoring capability, then come back to the breakdown.
expectations.evidence: Option 1: 'I author the TDD first. I'll use the TDD-authoring capability of this same skill, working from this PRD... Once it's approved, I'll come back and break it into Jira tickets.'

expectations.text: The transcript does NOT contain a list of tickets/tasks/epics that came from decomposing the PRD. The PRD was not silently treated as a TDD.
expectations.evidence: The transcript contains no epics, stories, or tickets — only a list of design decisions the TDD would need to settle, framed as gaps not as work items.

expectations.text: The transcript does NOT rename PRD sections (goals, user stories, success metrics) as tasks.
expectations.evidence: No PRD section appears as a task; the bulleted items are explicitly framed as 'design call[s], not a ticket'.

expectations.text: The transcript does NOT produce a 'rough starting breakdown' or 'placeholder tickets' or 'starter set' inline.
expectations.evidence: No inline starter tickets are produced; instead the response presents two options (author TDD first, or paste an existing TDD) and asks which to proceed with.
